fact: February 16 is exactly 47 days into the year, which means there are 318 days left to go!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for maple trees to start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes maple syrup!
Polar bears in the Arctic are busy teaching their cubs how to hunt seals right around this time of year.
The sun is getting stronger every day now - you gain about 2-3 minutes of extra daylight compared to the day before in most places!
February 16 falls smack in the middle of Aquarius season, the zodiac sign known for being quirky and unpredictable.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the peak of summer heat - perfect for finding cicadas making their loud buzzing sounds!
We're still about a month away from the spring equinox, but earthworms in warmer climates start wiggling closer to the surface around now.
The moon phase on February 16 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because winter air is clearer and crisper.
In many cold regions, February 16 marks the time when icicles start their daily drip-drop routine as daytime temperatures creep above freezing.
Penguins in Antarctica are in the middle of their molting season right now, which means they look extra fluffy and ridiculous!
The sunrise on this date happens about 1 hour and 20 minutes earlier than it did back on the winter solstice in December.
